prompt_text: Open source or affordable CRM tools instead of Pipedrive
response:
If you want to move away from Pipedrive, you have two main directions: **self‑hosted open source CRMs (very low license cost, more setup)** and **hosted, affordable CRMs (SaaS, like Pipedrive but cheaper or with good free tiers)**.

Below is a concise, practical shortlist.

---

## 1. Open source CRMs (self‑hosted / very low cost)

Best if you have some technical capacity (or an IT partner) and want to avoid per‑user SaaS fees over time.

### SuiteCRM
- **Type:** Mature, full‑featured open source CRM.[2][3][4][6]  
- **Why consider it:**  
  - Sales, marketing, support, workflows, reporting – fairly comparable “breadth” to Pipedrive, but less modern UI.[3][5]  
  - Strong community, many extensions, long track record.[3][5][6]  
  - **No per‑user license fees**; you just pay for hosting and maintenance.[5][6]  
- **Good for:** Teams that want a Pipedrive alternative with lots of features and can tolerate an older UX in exchange for cost savings.

### EspoCRM
- **Type:** Modern, lightweight open source CRM, self‑hosted.[1][5][7]  
- **Why consider it:**  
  - Clean UI, deals, contacts, activities, email, customer portal, basic campaigns.[1][5]  
  - Known for being **developer‑friendly and easy to customize** (entities, workflows, layouts).[1][5][7]  
- **Good for:** SMEs that liked Pipedrive’s simplicity and pipeline focus, and want something lean that can grow with customizations.

### Twenty
- **Type:** Newer, GitHub‑centric open source CRM focusing on developer experience.[7][8]  
- **Why consider it:**  
  - Strong emphasis on **modern UX and “normal” dev workflow** (local setup, tests, CI, etc.).[7][8]  
  - Can be self‑hosted or used as a paid managed cloud.[8]  
- **Good for:** Startups/tech companies with in‑house developers who want a CRM they can treat like internal software.

### Odoo (CRM module)
- **Type:** Open source ERP with CRM module.[2][3][5]  
- **Why consider it:**  
  - CRM plus quotes, invoicing, inventory, projects, etc., in one system.[2][3][5]  
  - The CRM app can be free if you only use one app in the official cloud, or fully open source if self‑hosted.[5]  
- **Good for:** Businesses wanting CRM tightly integrated with operations (billing, inventory, etc.) instead of a standalone pipeline tool.

### VTiger (open core)
- **Type:** Mix of open source and SaaS; CRM with sales, marketing, support.[2][3][5]  
- **Why consider it:**  
  - Recognized as a solid option for growing teams; has free/open variants and a hosted edition.[2][3][5]  
- **Good for:** Teams that might start self‑hosted and later move to a managed/paid plan for convenience.

Other open‑source names worth a look, depending on your use case: **Flectra, X2CRM, Corteza/Crust, YetiForce**.[5]  

---

## 2. Affordable hosted alternatives (if you don’t want to self‑host)

Your query mentions “open source or affordable,” so here are *hosted* CRMs that are not necessarily open source but are often cheaper or have usable free tiers. (Details vary by provider and plan; these are broad characterizations based on overviews.[2][3][5])

- **Odoo Online (CRM app only)** – Free for 1 app; you pay only if you add more apps or users beyond the free tier.[5]  
- **VTiger Cloud** – Lower‑priced than many enterprise CRMs, with plans geared at small and growing teams; offers free limits for small setups.[3][5]  
- **SuiteCRM / EspoCRM managed hosting** – Some vendors provide hosted versions so you pay a flat hosting fee but keep open‑source flexibility.[5][6][1]  

If “affordable” for you mainly means **lower monthly spend and minimal IT work**, a hosted plan of Odoo, VTiger, or a managed SuiteCRM/EspoCRM can be a good middle ground.

---

## How to choose quickly

If you tell me:
- team size,
- whether you can self‑host (yes/no),
- must‑have features vs. Pipedrive (e.g., deal pipelines, email sync, automation, reporting, mobile app),
